#----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# libs/Einsums/Python — Python integration support headers and the
# autogenerated binding aggregator.
#
# This module is header-only and ALWAYS built: Annotations.hpp is included
# transitively from public headers (e.g. Einsums/Logging.hpp) regardless of
# EINSUMS_BUILD_PYTHON, so it must always be installed so a `find_package(Einsums)`
# consumer can compile downstream code. Its macros expand to `[[clang::annotate(...)]]`
# under Clang and to nothing on every other compiler, so the include is a free
# no-op in non-codegen builds.
#
# The autogen pipeline (per-module Python extensions named ``PyEinsums_<modulename>``)
# is wired up by cmake/Einsums_FinalizePybind.cmake when EINSUMS_BUILD_PYTHON
# is set; the apiary / pybind11 sanity checks below also only fire
# under that flag.

set(PythonHeaders
    Einsums/Python/Annotations.hpp
    Einsums/Python/Protocol.hpp
)

set(PythonSources)

include(Einsums_AddModule)
einsums_add_module(
  Einsums Python
  SOURCES ${PythonSources}
  HEADERS ${PythonHeaders}
  DEPENDENCIES
  MODULE_DEPENDENCIES
)

if(EINSUMS_BUILD_PYTHON)
    # apiary is built under external/apiary. The root CMakeLists
    # guards add_subdirectory on the same option, so the target should exist
    # by the time we reach here. Bail with a clear message if it doesn't.
    if(NOT TARGET apiary)
        message(FATAL_ERROR
            "EINSUMS_BUILD_PYTHON=ON but the apiary target is not defined. "
            "Make sure external/apiary is added before libs/."
        )
    endif()

    if(NOT TARGET pybind11::module)
        message(FATAL_ERROR "EINSUMS_BUILD_PYTHON=ON requires pybind11.")
    endif()

    # Phase 5 ships per-module Python extensions: each einsums_add_module that
    # passes ``PYBIND`` produces a target ``PyEinsums_<modulename>`` linking
    # the codegen output. Phase 7 will add a hand-written EinsumAdapter.cpp
    # here for the einsum runtime adapter.
endif()
